title: Quietly optimized
description:
One of the original performance modpacks of Modrinth. 

Pushing the limits of Minecraft.
body:
## Why Quietly optmized? (Qo)

Quietly Optimized (Otherwise just QO) is your go to performance modpack. One of the originals performance modpacks on Modrinth with a rich history.
Pushing minecraft to its very limits.

> Performance increases (Up to 500% over vanilla)

> Network stability improvements (Makes playing on servers way more stable)

> And many quality of life mods to improve your gameplay.

> Speeds up loading times (By a lot for my system)

> World loading time decreases

> Other micro optimizations

> Frequent updates! (Make sure to follow so you never miss them)

> Updated rendering engine (OpenGL 4.0+ courtesy of GPUBooster)  



## My recommended launchers

>ATLauncher - One of the BEST launchers you can use.
>https://atlauncher.com/

>MultiMC - Rock solid launcher for those who want a more stable experience.
>https://multimc.org/
> 
>Modrinth's app. The official Modrinth launcher. Its pretty solid. And open source.
>https://modrinth.com/app

## NOT RECOMMENDED LAUNCHERS 

>Prism Launcher - Hostile fork of PolyMC, current owners tired to force the old repo owner out. 

>PolyMC - Pretty much dead, you're better off using MultiMC or ATLauncher.



Quilt information -
While this modpack technically unofficially supports quilt, it is not recommended for three main reasons.

1 : The projects goal is to remove fabric support entirely, thus splitting the modding scene further. *Though they have wiped this off their website, I still have a feeling that this is the underlying goal.*

2 : Generally a useless fork of fabric, which was created from drama that was so petty it could've been resolved privately.
